title: Emit diagnostics for ERROR nodes and silent drops in lower_connect_body
analysis:
Task 396 addresses three silent-failure paths in lower_connect_body (ts_parser.rs:1329-1419). Prior work has already resolved two of the three issues: (1) an explicit "ERROR" arm was added at line 1405 to emit diagnostics for tree-sitter ERROR nodes, and (2) has_error() guards were added on the connect_param_assignment arm (line 1337) and port_mapping arm (line 1375). Both integration tests (in connect_chain_tests.rs, task-396 steps 1/3/5/7/8/10) and unit tests (in ts_parser.rs tests module, lines 2942-2998) already exist and verify these behaviors.

The remaining issue is the `_ => {}` catch-all at line 1414, which silently discards any child node that doesn't match the known arms. While tree-sitter's grammar for connect_body only produces connect_param_assignment, port_mapping, and ERROR as named children (plus anonymous punctuation and extras like comments), the catch-all should defensively emit diagnostics for unexpected named non-extra children — matching the robustness pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.

Key concern: comments (line_comment, block_comment) are tree-sitter extras that appear as named children of any node. The fix must use child.is_extra() to avoid spurious diagnostics on comments. The catch-all should only emit diagnostics when child.is_named() && !child.is_extra().

The defensive catch-all cannot be triggered through the current grammar under normal parsing, but CAN be tested by deliberately calling lower_connect_body on a wrong node type (e.g., a constraint_definition node), which produces named children like param_declaration and constraint_def_predicate that hit the catch-all.

prerequisites.description: ERROR arm, has_error() guards, unit tests, and integration tests already exist from prior work on this task. The "ERROR" arm at line 1405, has_error() guards at lines 1337 and 1375, unit tests (lower_connect_body_error_node_emits_diagnostic, lower_connect_body_malformed_param_emits_diagnostic, lower_connect_body_malformed_mapping_emits_diagnostic), and integration tests (connect_body_error_node_emits_diagnostic, connect_body_malformed_param_emits_diagnostic, connect_body_malformed_mapping_emits_diagnostic, connect_body_valid_no_spurious_errors, connect_statement_malformed_outer_emits_diagnostic, connect_statement_valid_outer_no_spurious_errors) are all committed and passing.

steps.description: Write integration test `connect_body_with_comment_no_spurious_errors` in connect_chain_tests.rs. Parse `structure S { port a : out T  port b : in T  connect a -> b : BoltSet { /* inline comment */ grade = 8.8 } }` and assert errors.is_empty(). Also test with line comments: `structure S { port a : out T  port b : in T  connect a -> b { // comment\nshaft -> bore } }`. Verifies comments inside connect body don't produce spurious diagnostics. This test should PASS with existing code (catch-all silently skips comments) and must continue to pass after the catch-all fix.

Verify step-1 test passes without code changes. The existing `_ => {}` catch-all correctly skips comment nodes since they don't match any arm. No implementation change needed — run `cargo test -p reify-syntax connect_body_with_comment` to confirm.

Write unit test `lower_connect_body_catch_all_emits_for_unexpected_named_children` in ts_parser.rs tests module. Parse `constraint Eq(a: Scalar, b: Scalar) { a == b }`, use find_node_by_kind to find the constraint_definition node, then call lower_connect_body on it. The constraint_definition's children include named non-extra nodes (identifier, param_declaration, constraint_def_predicate) that don't match any connect_body arm, so they hit the catch-all. Assert that lowering.errors is non-empty and at least one error message contains "unexpected". This test FAILS with current `_ => {}` (no errors emitted for unexpected named children).

Replace the `_ => {}` catch-all at line 1414 in lower_connect_body with a diagnostic-emitting version. Change to: `_ => { if child.is_named() && !child.is_extra() { self.errors.push(ParseError { message: format!("unexpected '{}' in connect body: {}", child.kind(), self.node_text(child)), span: self.span(child) }); } }`. This emits diagnostics for unexpected named non-extra children while still silently skipping anonymous punctuation tokens ({, }, ,) and extras (line_comment, block_comment). Step-3 test now PASSES.

Write unit test `lower_connect_body_extras_not_flagged` in ts_parser.rs tests module. Call lower_body_directly (existing helper) on `structure S { port a : out T  port b : in T  connect a -> b { /* comment */ grade = 8.8 } }`. Assert that no error message contains "unexpected". This verifies that the new catch-all check correctly skips extras (comments) via !child.is_extra(). This test should PASS with the step-4 implementation.

Verify step-5 passes with no additional changes. The is_extra() check in step-4 correctly filters comment nodes. Run full test suite (`cargo test -p reify-syntax`) to confirm no regressions across all 9 pre-existing task-396 tests plus the 3 new tests.

design_decisions.decision: Use child.is_extra() to filter tree-sitter extras (comments) rather than checking specific kind names like 'line_comment' or 'block_comment'
design_decisions.rationale: is_extra() is maintained by the tree-sitter grammar's extras array, so if new extras are added to the grammar (e.g., new comment styles), they'll be automatically filtered without updating the parser code. This is more maintainable than hardcoding kind names.

design_decisions.decision: Test the defensive catch-all by deliberately passing a wrong node type (constraint_definition) to lower_connect_body
design_decisions.rationale: The catch-all cannot be triggered through the current grammar — tree-sitter's error recovery always wraps unexpected content in ERROR nodes, which are caught by the explicit ERROR arm. Passing a wrong node type is the only reliable way to produce named non-extra children that hit the catch-all, and it tests the defensive behavior that protects against future grammar changes or unusual error recovery scenarios.

design_decisions.decision: Keep scope limited to lower_connect_body only; do NOT fix lower_port_body which has the same issues (no ERROR arm, silent catch-all at line 1267)
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly targets lower_connect_body. lower_port_body should be addressed in a separate task to maintain clean scope boundaries. Noted for follow-up.

design_decisions.decision: Use 'unexpected' as the keyword in the catch-all diagnostic message rather than 'syntax error' or 'invalid'
design_decisions.rationale: Distinguishes defensive catch-all diagnostics from tree-sitter ERROR node diagnostics ('syntax error in connect body') and has_error() guard diagnostics ('invalid connect parameter'). Each error path produces a uniquely identifiable message, making test assertions precise and debugging clearer.

reuse.how: Reuse for finding constraint_definition node in the catch-all test (step-3). Already exists and handles recursive tree traversal.
Reuse for step-5 test (extras not flagged). Parses source, finds connect_body node, calls lower_connect_body directly, returns errors.
Reuse for step-1 integration test. Parses source and returns (declarations, errors) tuple.

reuse.what: Existing ParseError emit pattern from ERROR arm
reuse.how: Follow same pattern for the catch-all diagnostic: push ParseError with descriptive message and span from self.span(child). Keeps error emission consistent across all arms.

reuse.what: Lowering::new constructor for direct unit tests
reuse.how: Step-3 test creates a Lowering instance directly (same pattern as lower_body_directly helper) to call lower_connect_body on an arbitrary node type.
